text:
My practice explores the human body not merely as a biological entity, but as a site where the psychological, cosmic, and material collide. I once grappled with a Gnostic view of the body as a "prison" for the soul. My recent work, however, moves beyond this rejection to map the body as a sacred geography, connecting our internal microcosm to the wider universe.

In painting, I treat the canvas as a surface of psychological resistance, prioritizing atmosphere over likeness. In Reflection / Duality, I use paint\'s materiality to give weight to invisible states, building up the surface to capture the self's fragmented anxiety. This lens extends to the environment. In Chicago Skyline, I abandon architectural rigidity for the fluidity of ink on rice paper. Letting the cityscape bleed and dissolve, I transform the metropolis into an internal psychological space---capturing a fleeting atmosphere rather than a static document.

This inquiry led to research-based territory. Medical Astrology reimagines anatomy through a mystic lens, mapping Zodiac signs onto organs to visualize a "divine spark" within. This groundwork compelled an expansion into three-dimensional space. I see no boundary between representation and experience; my installation Respiration is an extension of my painting. By translating the invisible energy of breath into shared physical reality, I invite viewers to experience the connectivity I explore.
